title: PostgreSQL Integration, Loopback HTTP, Fault Injection, and Reconciliation · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs postgresql integration, loopback http, fault injection, and reconciliation from revision, tenant, source URL, request validators, response bytes, parsed identity, transaction, lease, state transition, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for disposable end-to-end ingestion harness after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Start an isolated compatible PostgreSQL 18 database, apply real migrations, seed original data, isolate tests, and prove teardown.
Use httptest origins and custom transports for validators, redirects, DNS decisions, compression, slow bodies, malformed bytes, disconnects, and publisher budgets.
Inject constraint, serialization, deadlock, lock wait, pool exhaustion, cancellation, connection loss, and commit ambiguity with expected recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Feed Go Disposable Postgres · changed case · Review Feed Go Disposable Post…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go team handles case 6036 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove disposable PostgreSQL, controlled origins, process behavior, injected faults, and end-to-end reconciliation; every goroutine, body, row set, transaction, lease, timer, and pool needs named cleanup ownership; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Start an isolated compatible PostgreSQL 18 database, apply real migrations, seed original data, isolate tests, and prove teardown.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Go team handles case 6834 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove disposable PostgreSQL, controlled origins, process behavior, injected faults, and end-to-end reconciliation; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Reconcile HTTP bytes, parsed entries, committed rows, versions, validators, queue state, API output, telemetry, and cleanup after success and failure.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Matching one API response proves the ingestion pipeline.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconcile HTTP bytes, parsed entries, committed rows, versions, validators, queue state, API output, telemetry, and cleanup after success and failure. Artifact under review: disposable end-to-end ingestion harness.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Feed Go E2e Reconciliation, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, import one convenient trusted RSS feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, XML and HTTP budgets, PostgreSQL constraints and transaction, schedule, lease, goroutine cleanup, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as feed-service proof.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go E2e Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Go E2e Reconciliation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 5215 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove disposable PostgreSQL, controlled origins, process behavior, injected faults, and end-to-end reconciliation; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Reconcile HTTP bytes, parsed entries, committed rows, versions, validators, queue state, API output, telemetry, and cleanup after success and failure.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go E2e Reconciliation.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Feed aggregation and Go Record what required effort · Review Feed Go E2e Reconciliation, keep Feed Go E2e Reconciliation connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Reconcile HTTP bytes, parsed entries, committed rows, versions, validators, queue state, API output, telemetry, and cleanup after success and failure.
